source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure CloudTrail logs are encrypted at rest using KMS CMKs

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure CloudTrail logs are encrypted at rest using KMS CMKs
input_row.description: AWS CloudTrail is a web service that records AWS API calls for an account and makes those logs available to users and resources in accordance with IAM policies. AWS Key Management Service (KMS) is a managed service that helps create and control the encryption keys used to encrypt account data, and uses Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) to protect the security of encryption keys. CloudTrail logs can be configured to leverage server side encryption (SSE) and KMS customer-created master keys (CMK) to further protect CloudTrail logs. It is recommended that CloudTrail be configured to use SSE-KMS.
input_row.rationale: Configuring CloudTrail to use SSE-KMS provides additional confidentiality controls on log data, as a given user must have S3 read permission on the corresponding log bucket and must be granted decrypt permission by the CMK policy. Impact: Customer-created keys incur an additional cost. See https://aws.amazon.com/kms/pricing/ for more information.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if CloudTrail is configured to use SSE-KMS: From Console: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the CloudTrail console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudtrail. 2. In the left navigation pane, choose Trails. 3. Select a trail. 4. In the General details section, select Edit to edit the trail configuration. 5. Ensure the box at Log file SSE-KMS encryption is checked and that a valid AWS KMS alias of a KMS key is entered in the respective text box. From Command Line: 1. Run the following command: aws cloudtrail describe-trails  2. For each trail listed, SSE-KMS is enabled if the trail has a KmsKeyId property defined.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to configure CloudTrail to use SSE-KMS: From Console: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the CloudTrail console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/cloudtrail. 2. In the left navigation pane, choose Trails. 3. Click on a trail. 4. Under the S3 section, click the edit button (pencil icon). 5. Click Advanced. 6. Select an existing CMK from the KMS key Id drop-down menu. • Note: Ensure the CMK is located in the same region as the S3 bucket. • Note: You will need to apply a KMS key policy on the selected CMK in order for CloudTrail, as a service, to encrypt and decrypt log files using the CMK provided. View the AWS documentation for editing the selected CMK Key policy. 7. Click Save. 8. You will see a notification message stating that you need to have decryption permissions on the specified KMS key to decrypt log files. 9. Click Yes. From Command Line: Run the following command to specify a KMS key ID to use with a trail: aws cloudtrail update-trail --name <trail-name>  --kms-id <cloudtrail-kms- key> Run the following command to attach a key policy to a specified KMS key: aws kms put-key-policy --key-id <cloudtrail-kms-key> --policy <cloudtrail- kms-key-policy> References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/encrypting- cloudtrail-log-files-with-aws-kms.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/create-keys.html 3. CCE-78919-8 4. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/cloudtrail/u pdate-trail.html 5. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/kms/put- key-policy.html Additional Information: Three statements that need to be added to the CMK policy: 1. Enable CloudTrail to describe CMK properties: <pre class="programlisting" style="font-style: normal;">{ "Sid": "Allow CloudTrail access", "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "Service": "cloudtrail.amazonaws.com" }, "Action": "kms:DescribeKey", "Resource": "*" } 2. Granting encrypt permissions: <pre class="programlisting" style="font-style: normal;">{ "Sid": "Allow CloudTrail to encrypt logs", "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "Service": "cloudtrail.amazonaws.com" }, "Action": "kms:GenerateDataKey*", "Resource": "*", "Condition": { "StringLike": { "kms:EncryptionContext:aws:cloudtrail:arn": [ "arn:aws:cloudtrail:*:aws-account-id:trail/*" ] } } } 3. Granting decrypt permissions: <pre class="programlisting" style="font-style: normal;">{ "Sid": "Enable CloudTrail log decrypt permissions", "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::aws-account-id:user/username" }, "Action": "kms:Decrypt", "Resource": "*", "Condition": { "Null": { "kms:EncryptionContext:aws:cloudtrail:arn": "false" } } }
input_row.impact: Customer-created keys incur an additional cost. See https://aws.amazon.com/kms/pricing/ for more information.
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/encrypting- cloudtrail-log-files-with-aws-kms.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/create-keys.html 3. CCE-78919-8 4. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/cloudtrail/u pdate-trail.html 5. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/kms/put- key-policy.html Additional Information: Three statements that need to be added to the CMK policy: 1. Enable CloudTrail to describe CMK properties: <pre class="programlisting" style="font-style: normal;">{ "Sid": "Allow CloudTrail access", "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "Service": "cloudtrail.amazonaws.com" }, "Action": "kms:DescribeKey", "Resource": "*" } 2. Granting encrypt permissions: <pre class="programlisting" style="font-style: normal;">{ "Sid": "Allow CloudTrail to encrypt logs", "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "Service": "cloudtrail.amazonaws.com" }, "Action": "kms:GenerateDataKey*", "Resource": "*", "Condition": { "StringLike": { "kms:EncryptionContext:aws:cloudtrail:arn": [ "arn:aws:cloudtrail:*:aws-account-id:trail/*" ] } } } 3. Granting decrypt permissions: <pre class="programlisting" style="font-style: normal;">{ "Sid": "Enable CloudTrail log decrypt permissions", "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::aws-account-id:user/username" }, "Action": "kms:Decrypt", "Resource": "*", "Condition": { "Null": { "kms:EncryptionContext:aws:cloudtrail:arn": "false" } } }

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control is related to AWS CloudTrail, a standard AWS service. CloudTrail logs configuration, including encryption settings, can be accessed and verified using AWS API calls.
- The presence of a `KmsKeyId` property in the trail's configuration is explicit in determining whether the logs are encrypted using KMS CMKs.
- The audit section explicitly mentions using AWS CLI commands (`aws cloudtrail describe-trails`) to verify the existence of the `KmsKeyId` property.
- Boto3 SDK can effectively be used to retrieve this information programmatically.
- This control involves standard AWS features (CloudTrail, KMS) that are accessible via AWS APIs, indicating that an automated approach is feasible and efficient.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_encryption_kms_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API Calls and boto3 SDK Methods**: Use `boto3` to invoke `cloudtrail.describe_trails()` method to get details of all trails in the account.
- **AWS CLI Commands**: Use `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` command to list all trails, and check for the `KmsKeyId` property in each trail’s configuration.
- **Resources to Enumerate**: All CloudTrail trails across the account, iterating across all configured AWS regions.
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**:
  - Ensure that each trail returned by `describe_trails` has the `KmsKeyId` property defined.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
  - **Pass**: The trail has a `KmsKeyId` property defined, indicating that the trail is configured to use SSE-KMS encryption.
  - **Fail**: The trail does not have a `KmsKeyId` property, indicating that the trail is not using SSE-KMS encryption.

This approach ensures that all CloudTrail trails are systematically checked for SSE-KMS encryption, utilizing AWS APIs for accuracy and efficiency.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. AWS CloudTrail configurations, including encryption settings, can be accessed and verified using AWS APIs, making automation feasible.
- The use of AWS SDK (boto3) and AWS CLI to check for the `KmsKeyId` property in CloudTrail configurations is appropriate and supported by AWS API capabilities.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The `boto3` method `cloudtrail.describe_trails()` and the AWS CLI command `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` are valid and correctly used to retrieve trail configurations.
- The pass/fail criteria based on the presence of the `KmsKeyId` property is correct for determining if SSE-KMS encryption is enabled.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_encryption_kms_enabled` is descriptive and follows a reasonable naming convention, though it could be slightly more specific by including "at_rest" to clarify the encryption context.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- Consider iterating over all AWS regions to ensure no trails are missed, as CloudTrail can be region-specific.
- The program name could be improved to `aws_cloudtrail_encryption_kms_at_rest_enabled` for greater clarity.
- It might be beneficial to include a check for the status of the KMS key (e.g., whether it is enabled) to ensure the encryption is fully operational.
- Consider using AWS Config rules for continuous compliance monitoring, such as `cloudtrail-log-file-validation-enabled` and `cloudtrail-enabled`.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, leveraging AWS API capabilities effectively. The technical details are accurate, and the approach is comprehensive for the stated control.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `aws_cloudtrail_encryption_kms_at_rest_enabled` for clarity.
- Consider adding checks for the KMS key status and exploring AWS Config for ongoing compliance monitoring.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identified that AWS CloudTrail encryption settings, specifically the use of KMS CMKs for log file encryption at rest, are fully accessible via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The presence of the `KmsKeyId` property in the CloudTrail trail configuration is definitive and can be programmatically checked across all regions. No manual inspection is required, as all relevant data is exposed through standard AWS SDK methods.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_cloudtrail_trail_encryption_kms_at_rest_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: CloudTrail Trails
- API/CLI Method: boto3 cloudtrail.describe_trails(), aws cloudtrail describe-trails
- Properties to Check: For each trail, verify the presence and non-null value of the `KmsKeyId` property.
- Pass Criteria: Every CloudTrail trail in every region has a `KmsKeyId` property set (i.e., not null/empty), indicating SSE-KMS encryption at rest is enabled.
- Fail Criteria: Any CloudTrail trail is missing the `KmsKeyId` property or it is null/empty, indicating logs are not encrypted at rest with a customer-managed KMS CMK.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The only minor difference was in the suggested program name; I have selected a more descriptive and specific name (`aws_cloudtrail_trail_encryption_kms_at_rest_enabled`) to clearly indicate the control's intent and context.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
